name: Wall Run
description: A traversal state where the player runs along a wall for a limited distance or window.
design_notes: Wall run requires strict surface eligibility, camera support, gravity modification, and exit rules. It should feel like controlled momentum, not an animation glued to a wall.
design_purpose: Create high-mobility paths through combat or platform spaces.
Reward approach angle, momentum, and route planning.
player_fantasy: The player feels athletic and cinematic, using vertical surfaces as temporary ground.

parameters.name: wall_run_min_entry_velocity
parameters.description: Minimum incoming movement needed to attach to a wall run.

parameters.name: wall_run_gravity_scale
parameters.description: Gravity multiplier while attached to the wall.

parameters.name: wall_run_exit_impulse
parameters.description: Launch impulse applied when jumping away from the wall.

edge_cases: Curved or uneven walls can rapidly change normals and eject the player.
Wall run into corners should choose transition, stop, or jump behavior.
Camera roll and FOV effects should clear on forced exit or damage.
common_bugs: The player sticks to any wall because surface tags are not checked.
Gravity suppression remains active after leaving the wall.
Exit jump uses camera direction instead of wall normal and launches unpredictably.
balancing_notes: Long wall runs can replace level routes unless surfaces and stamina are constrained.
Entry velocity requirements make wall run a momentum skill instead of a universal climb.
accessibility_notes: Provide an option to reduce camera roll and use strong surface readability for runnable walls.

implementation_notes.unity: Detect runnable wall normals with capsule casts, project velocity along the wall tangent, apply wall_run_gravity_scale while attached, and exit when distance, angle, or input rules fail.
implementation_notes.godot: Use raycasts or shape casts from CharacterBody3D to cache the wall normal, then move along the tangent in _physics_process while reducing gravity and tracking max distance.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a custom CharacterMovement mode for wall running so gravity, braking, camera roll, and jump-off impulse are owned by movement code instead of animation montages.
implementation_notes.web: Represent wall-run attachment as an actor state with wall_normal, entry_velocity, and distance_traveled, then project motion along the surface before resolving collisions.

example_games.title: Titanfall 2
example_games.note: Uses wall running as a core traversal and combat-positioning tool.

example_games.title: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time
example_games.note: Uses wall running as part of acrobatic traversal routes.
